title: Transferability for General Reasoning: An Automated Curriculum for Multi-Domain RLVR
author: Yongjin Yang, Jiarui Liu, Yinghui He, Lezhen Zhang, Bernhard Sch\"olkopf, Zhijing Jin
raw_description: arXiv:2606.25178v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has been extended from single-domain training to multi-domain reasoning suites spanning mathematics, programming, and science. However, the training curriculum (how often each domain is sampled) is typically fixed or hand-tuned, even though reasoning skills transfer unevenly across domains. Existing learnability-based curricula adapt to where the policy is currently improving, but are blind to whether a gradient step on the selected domain benefits the remaining domains. In this paper, we propose Transfer-Aware Curriculum (TAC), a bandit-style online curriculum that prioritizes domains whose updates broadly benefit the rest of the training suite. TAC repurposes signals already produced by RL training: per-domain advantages capture local learnability, and projected gradients, taken from the GRPO step being computed, estimate cross-domain transferability via gradient-geometry alignment,
summary: 提出Transfer-Aware Curriculum (TAC)，一种基于多领域RLVR的在线课程学习方法，通过梯度几何对齐自动选择能最大化跨领域迁移收益的训练领域。
analysis_note: 针对RLVR中课程设计盲点提出创新方法，理论扎实且实用性强，属于关键技术突破。
